metrics.narrative: No buyer-quantified figures (cost, cycle time, savings) have surfaced on any call; the pain case is qualitative. MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 2026-05-15) frames it as fragmented, manual supplier-facing processes across HOLDCO/OPCOs and the need for one auditable platform to support direct/defense procurement, localization, local content, SDP and IPP. The move to quantify (cycle-time or compliance-risk reduction figures) has not yet been made a deliverable.

champion.narrative: No champion is identified. MEDDPICC 2.0 is explicit: 'Very arms-length process - Carl has had positive conversations with Franck, but he doesn't fit the champion role.' Franck Devienne has been consistently present (21 May 2026 clarification request, 6 Jun 2026 ongoing clarifications, 23 Jun 2026 demo attendance) but functions as a process gatekeeper, not an internal advocate driving Zycus's case with the decision-makers above him. No internal introduction, shared internal document, or pushback-for-Zycus-against-a-rival has been evidenced on any call.

competition.narrative: Four named rivals: SAP Ariba, GEP Worldwide, iValua, Coupa (Competitors__c; corroborated by MEDDPICC 2.0, 2026-05-15: 'Ariba (relationship with SAP/IBM...), iValua (heavy customisation/localisation), GEP (heavy services support), Coupa (strong A&D customers)'). SAP Ariba is the standing current threat: SAMI runs S/4HANA and its 23 Jun 2026 demo raised open questions on consignment/MRP-S/4HANA integration that Zycus has not yet closed with proof (Saudi/S/4 implementation examples remain an overdue action item). Zycus's wedge is its deeper AI/agentic story (Merlin, ANA autonomous negotiation agent, intake management) demonstrated in depth on 13 Jan 2026, activated by delivering the overdue Saudi/S/4HANA integration proof points before the end-July 2026 evaluation conclusion.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated in MEDDPICC 2.0: fragmented and manual supplier-facing processes spanning onboarding, qualification, sourcing and development; lack of transparency and standardized supplier data across SAMI's HOLDCO/OPCO structure; and the need to support direct/defense procurement, localization, local content, SDP and IPP in one auditable platform. On the 13 Jan 2026 call, Carl (Zycus) reinforced this via the ST Engineering reference case, describing the same manual/silo pain the reference customer had solved. The pain owner is the SAMI procurement function (Franck Devienne's team).

paper_process.narrative: No MSA/SOW, legal, or InfoSec-formal-review artifacts have surfaced yet; the deal is still in evaluation, not contracting. The one paper-adjacent signal is that revised commercial quotes must be routed through SI partners KPMG and Accenture per SAMI's request (3 Jul 2026 Next Step), which will add a partner-mediated layer to any eventual paper process. Visibility would be created by asking Franck directly what contracting vehicle (direct MSA vs. SI-mediated framework) SAMI intends to use post-award.

economic_buyer.narrative: MEDDPICC 2.0 names Franck Devienne, Senior Procurement Advisor, as economic buyer, and budget is described as 'centrally held, but Franck and team will determine the award decision.' Franck has been the consistent point of contact (attended the 23 Jun 2026 demo, reached out for on-prem/cloud clarification on 21 May 2026), but no CFO/CPO-level or board-level sponsor has appeared on any call, and the same CRM record separately flags this as 'a very arms-length process.' Treat Franck as the de facto commercial gatekeeper, with true budget authority above him still unconfirmed.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a clean, traceable sequence: NDA (15 Dec 2025) -> RFI (16 Dec 2025, submitted 24 Jan 2026) -> Innovation Contest presentations (22-26 Feb 2026, Zycus session ~24 Feb 2026) -> confidential down-select to 3 vendors (2 Mar 2026) -> RFP issued (13 Mar 2026, extended to 21 Apr 2026) -> RFP submitted with ~600 clarification rounds (4-13 May 2026) -> live use-case demo (23 Jun 2026) -> stated final decision round around mid-July 2026 and evaluation conclusion by end of July 2026 (23 Jun 2026 demo notes; 3 Jul 2026 Next Step). All further meetings are gated through CK (Carl Kimball) in the Middle East per Next_Step_History__c.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented: functional/technical compliance, cybersecurity, SAP/S/4HANA integration, A&D/direct-procurement/MRO fit, local content/LCGPA/SDP/IPP fit, KSA implementation/support capability, knowledge transfer, a filled compliance matrix and the commercial response (SF MEDDPICC_2_0__c). The 23 Jun 2026 live demo tested these directly: Zycus walked through eRFX, supplier portal/onboarding, local-content dashboards, catalog management and the PR-to-GRN lifecycle, and the buyer's technical gatekeeper probed integration and defense-compliance specifics in depth.

items.name: Carl's status update on SI partners + revised DC slide
items.summary: Carl replied same-day with the AEC data warehouse slide update and a partner-by-partner status: EY nearly done reconciling teams, KPMG committing to finish in 1-2 days, and Accenture again declining to bid alone (wants to pair with another ecosystem partner SAMI hasn't approved); final implementation commercials are on hold until all proposals and joint costs are finalized.

day_summary.overall: SAMI's Yousef chased Zycus (again) for the overdue SI vendor proposals and AEC hosting details, having expected them 'early this week' but still empty-handed by Thursday. Carl finally responded same-day with a substantive status update: EY's proposal is nearly finalized, KPMG has committed to finishing in 1-2 days, but Accenture has again refused to bid solo (insisting on partnering with another ecosystem vendor SAMI doesn't want). Carl also flagged that final implementation commercials can't be locked until all SI proposals land and joint costs are optimized — so the deal remains stalled on Zycus's side pending partner deliverables, with SAMI now visibly frustrated at the slipping timeline.

cro_panel.intro: SAMI is a live, engaged $2.2M defense-sector RFP deal shortlisted to a final field of three, with a detailed and well-documented evaluation trail, but it carries two structural gaps (no confirmed executive economic buyer/champion, and an unproven integration story against the SAP-native incumbent option) that keep it from reading as a clean win, plus a close date that looks tighter than the buyer's own timeline supports

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: SAMI is running a formal, government-grade RFP evaluating a confidential shortlist of 3 finalists out of an original field that included SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP Worldwide, and Coupa.

bullets.full: No EB engagement, no champion. Franck is gatekeeper; CFO/CPO-level budget authority is unmapped and has not joined a call. No advocacy, no EB access, no inside intel on competitor positioning. Ariba likely has executive-level SAP/IBM relationships Zycus lacks.

AI data-residency risk: MEDDPICC 2.0 blocker states 'ZERO AI functionality will be accepted' due to cybersecurity/KSA national-security/data-residency restrictions. If on-prem/private-cloud AI is not viable, Zycus loses its only differentiation vs Ariba/iValua/GEP and becomes a commoditized feature/price fight.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and engaged (buyer email reply 7 Jul 2026, Next Step fresh as of 3 Jul 2026), but two Zycus-owed deliverables are overdue and no champion/EB has emerged, so momentum toward the end-July 2026 decision is real but fragile.

north_star_verdict.math: 52 days to close (2026-08-29) as of 8 Jul 2026. Deal has sat in Shortlisted since SAMI confirmed the down-select to 3 vendors on 2 Mar 2026 (~128 days in stage), moving through RFP submission (4 May 2026), ~600 clarifications (13 May 2026), and a live SRM demo (23 Jun 2026) that landed well on functional fit. SAMI's own stated timeline (23 Jun demo notes + 3 Jul 2026 Next Step) points to evaluation conclusion by end of July 2026 and a final decision round around mid-July 2026. Two Zycus-side deliverables (written defense-compliance responses; revised SI-partner quotes) have been outstanding since 23 Jun 2026 (~15 days as of today), stalling the run-up to that decision. If SAMI decides mid-to-late July, the 29 Aug 2026 close leaves only 4-6 weeks for SI-partner-mediated commercial negotiation, defense/InfoSec compliance close-out, and contracting on a $2.2M enterprise A&D deal.

north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline until SAMI's decision converts to a confirmed shortlist win (expected mid-to-late Jul 2026); if won, re-forecast close to Oct-Nov 2026 to allow for SI-partner commercial negotiation and defense/InfoSec compliance close-out.

flags: Single-threaded through gatekeeper Franck Devienne with no confirmed champion or executive budget authority mapped; use Carl Kimball's existing relationship and SI partners KPMG/Accenture to reach the true award-decision authority before the mid-July 2026 final round.

SAP Ariba's assumed S/4HANA/SAP-ecosystem integration edge is unanswered since the 23 Jun 2026 demo; supplying concrete Saudi/S/4HANA reference proof is the single highest-leverage move to close the credibility gap before evaluation concludes end of July 2026.

The MEDDPICC 2.0 blocker 'ZERO AI functionality will be accepted' threatens Zycus's only real differentiator (Merlin/Agentic AI); validate an on-prem/private-cloud AI deployment model now so this doesn't default to disqualification before the decision window.
Convert Franck Devienne from arms-length gatekeeper to advocate by arming him with a defensible written business case, since MEDDPICC 2.0 already flags he 'doesn't fit the champion role.'

No champion exists inside SAMI beyond the arms-length gatekeeper Franck Devienne; with the award decision expected mid-July 2026, cultivate an internal advocate among the technical/A&D stakeholders who joined the 23 Jun demo.

Zycus is currently the bottleneck on its own committed SI implementation proposal (KPMG/EY), chased three times by the buyer since 16 Jun 2026; closing this fast protects the 'good' technical position already earned in the demo.
best_practice_check.summary: Two overdue Zycus deliverables and an unmapped internal advocate are the biggest levers to close before SAMI's end-July 2026 decision.

customer_preference.evidence: Rep-reported 'our position it was good' on the 23 Jun demo (Next_Step__c, 3 Jul 2026); no direct buyer quote of preference over a named competitor was captured on any read call.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba is the single strongest current threat: SAMI runs S/4HANA, and MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-05-15) notes the buyer directly probes vendors on 'why not Ariba' given its SAP/IBM ecosystem tie; Zycus offered iSaaS middleware/adapters at the 23 Jun 2026 demo but has not yet supplied the requested recent Saudi/S/4HANA implementation proof, leaving the integration-parity question open. GEP Worldwide (heavy services support, a natural fit for SAMI's complex A&D rollout) and iValua (heavy customisation/localisation options relevant to KSA local-content needs) are live but secondary peer threats with no fresh 2026 evidence updating their standing. Coupa is the lowest current threat, cited mainly for its A&D customer base with no fresh signal of active traction at SAMI this sweep.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: SAMI is a live, engaged $2.2M defense-sector RFP deal shortlisted to a final field of three, with a detailed and well-documented evaluation trail, but it carries two structural gaps (no confirmed executive economic buyer/champion, and an unproven integration story against the SAP-native incumbent option) that keep it from reading as a clean win, plus a close date that looks tighter than the buyer's own timeline supports.

deal_risk.text: MEDDPICC 2.0 blockers flag 'ZERO AI functionality will be accepted' due to KSA cybersecurity/data-residency and national-security restrictions (2026-05-15) — if on-prem/private-cloud AI cannot be validated as compliant, Zycus loses its primary differentiator against Ariba/iValua/GEP.

The recorded 29 Aug 2026 close date is tight against SAMI's own stated end-of-July 2026 evaluation conclusion plus the standard defense contracting relay (InfoSec, legal, Saudization staffing terms) still ahead.

win_position.text: SAMI has confirmed Zycus is one of a confidential final-three shortlist since 2 Mar 2026, and the 23 Jun 2026 live SRM demo landed as a strong functional fit per the rep's own read (Next Step, 3 Jul 2026).

No CFO/CPO/executive-level economic buyer has joined any call to date; Franck Devienne is named EB in the CRM but is explicitly described as 'arms-length' and not a champion (MEDDPICC 2.0, 2026-05-15).
SAP Ariba's native S/4HANA ecosystem edge remains unrebutted: recent Saudi/S/4HANA integration proof points requested at the 23 Jun 2026 demo are still outstanding as of 9 Jul 2026.

deal_momentum.text: SAMI replied by email on 7 Jul 2026 (2 days ago), a genuine two-way buyer touch that keeps the clarification channel open ahead of its stated end-of-July 2026 evaluation conclusion.
A steady 7-month cadence of dated, forward-moving milestones (NDA/RFI Dec 2025 through live demo Jun 2026) shows sustained buyer-side process engagement, not drift.
The rep's 3 Jul 2026 outreach on revised SI-partner quotes is awaiting buyer reply; no confirmation yet that KPMG/Accenture routing has been completed.

customer_commitment.text: SAMI committed EY to prime the bid and supply 30% local Saudi resources as part of the RFP process (Next_Step_History__c, 25 Mar 2026), signaling real internal investment in a compliant path to award.
Buyer-voiced preference for Zycus specifically has not been captured in any read call; the 'good position' read on 3 Jul 2026 is the rep's own assessment, not a buyer quote.
